title: Allow --device-cgroup-rule to be passed in by docker API
body:
This looks like the correct fix, but I have no idea how to test.

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17106

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

Will have reporter verify if this fixes the problem.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
DeviceCgroupRules can now be used over Docker API.
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL
The test talked about in the PR involves using the docker executable and looking at configuration in the cgroup v1 database, so will not work with cgroup V2 as I understand it.
@giuseppe Do you have a way to check this on a CgroupV2 system?
Changes LGTM, let's wait for Giuseppe to weigh in on the test.

> @giuseppe Do you have a way to check this on a CgroupV2 system?

no, there is no way (that I know of) to do it on cgroupv2.  The cgroup device controller is handled with eBPF

Can we just use the e2e test: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/8f0a0f3ec38ca202a5fb18e6db3c3d107fa856de/test/e2e/run_test.go#L1546-L1555

I would just do the same via compat API.
I see no reference there that this is skipped on cgroupsv2.
I think that the test is fine.  the OCI runtime will convert the cgroupv1 rules into eBPF so we can verify inside the container if the device is accessible.
If someone wants to write a test for this, I will add it. I am a little under the weather right now. Some bad stomach virus going around.

I tried:
```
if root; then
  # compat api: bind mount without mount options
  payload='{"DeviceCgroupRules":[{"c","42:*","rwm"}],"CapAdd":"mknod"}'
  t POST containers/create Image=$IMAGE Cmd='["mknod","newDev","c","42","1"]' HostConfig="$payload" 201 .Id~[0-9a-f]\\{64\\}
  cid=$(jq -r '.Id' <<<"$output")
  podman start $cid
fi
```
But it fails with:
```
not ok 309 [20-containers] POST containers/create [-d {"Image":"quay.io/libpod/alpine_labels:latest","Cmd":["mknod","newDev","c","42","1"],"HostConfig":{"DeviceCgroupRules":[{"c","42:*","rwm"}],"CapAdd":"mknod"}}] : status
#  expected: 201
#    actual: 500
#  response: {"cause":"invalid character ',' after object key","message":"decode(): invalid character ',' after object key","response":500}
ok 310 [20-containers] POST containers/create [-d {"Image":"quay.io/libpod/alpine_labels:latest","Cmd":["mknod","newDev","c","42","1"],"HostConfig":{"DeviceCgroupRules":[{"c","42:*","rwm"}],"CapAdd":"mknod"}}]: .Id~[0-9a-f]\{64\} # skip - wrong return code
```

@podman-maintainers PTAL
I think we should merge this, I did check it with docker,
@jwhonce Could you help out with this?  How can I figure out what Docker is sending?

> payload='{"DeviceCgroupRules":[{"c","42:*","rwm"}],"CapAdd":"mknod"}'

Should this get you through parsing... `DeviceCgroupRules    []string`

 payload='{"DeviceCgroupRules":["c 42:* rwm"],"CapAdd":"mknod"}'

I did not trace the handler to determine if those  strings are parsed or ran through specgen pkg.
I can not get this to parse through our test matrix, can we just merge this and move on, it is a real corner case and seems to be an obvious fix.
/lgtm
/cherry-pick v4.4.0

@baude: cannot checkout `v4.4.0`: error checking out v4.4.0: exit status 1. output: error: pathspec 'v4.4.0' did not match any file(s) known to git


<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/17114#issuecomment-1405733528):

>/cherry-pick v4.4.0


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
/cherrypick v4.4

@baude: new pull request created: #17242

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/17114#issuecomment-1405741204):

>/cherrypick v4.4


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

Over on the issue manual testing was requested, and [it was reported](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17106#issuecomment-1396874699) that the change does not appear to be sufficient to fix the issue. I see the same when testing manually (on a cgroups v1 VM). Perhaps this was merged prematurely?
Open up a new issue on podman testing with the latest code.
